title: Running Tests
description: Master different ways to run Playwright tests including command line options, filtering, and test modes.
objectives: Run tests using various command line options
Filter and select specific tests to run
Use different execution modes (headed, UI, debug)

sections.title: Basic Test Execution
sections.code:
# Run all tests
npx playwright test

# Run tests in a specific file
npx playwright test login.spec.ts

# Run tests in a directory
npx playwright test tests/auth/

# Run tests with specific title
npx playwright test -g "login"

# Run tests in headed mode (see browser)
npx playwright test --headed

# Run in specific browser
npx playwright test --project=chromium
npx playwright test --project=firefox
npx playwright test --project=webkit

# Run in all browsers
npx playwright test --project=chromium --project=firefox --project=webkit
sections.explanation: These are the most common commands for running tests. Start with basic runs, then add options as needed.

sections.title: Test Filtering
sections.code:
# Filter by test name (grep)
npx playwright test -g "user can login"

# Multiple filters
npx playwright test -g "login|signup"

# Negative filter (exclude tests)
npx playwright test -g "login" -gv "social"

# Run only tests with specific tag
npx playwright test --grep @smoke

# Run failed tests from last run
npx playwright test --last-failed

# Run tests that match pattern
npx playwright test login signup

# Run single test by line number (in UI mode)
npx playwright test login.spec.ts:15
sections.explanation: Filtering allows you to run specific subsets of tests, which is useful during development.

sections.title: Execution Modes
sections.code:
# Headed mode - see browser
npx playwright test --headed

# UI mode - interactive test runner
npx playwright test --ui

# Debug mode - step through tests
npx playwright test --debug

# Debug specific test
npx playwright test login.spec.ts:15 --debug

# Headed with slow motion
npx playwright test --headed --slow-mo=1000

# Run in parallel (default)
npx playwright test --workers=4

# Run serially (one at a time)
npx playwright test --workers=1

# Run specific number of workers
npx playwright test --workers=50%
sections.explanation: Different modes help with debugging and understanding test execution.

keyTakeaways: npx playwright test runs all tests by default
Use --headed to see browser, --ui for interactive mode
Filter tests with -g flag or file/directory paths
Tests run in parallel by default for speed
--project flag runs tests in specific browsers
